title: Divergence In Chinas Coal Output And Coal Derived Electricity Generation
section_path: Divergence In Chinas Coal Output And Coal Derived Electricity Generation
section_path_text: Divergence In Chinas Coal Output And Coal Derived Electricity Generation
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
As we discussed in client notes last month and in Commodore Research's March 25th Weekly Dry Bulk Report, China's coal output averaged 352.6 million tons in January/February. This has marked a decline of 61.7 million tons (-15%) from December and is down year-on-year by 14.5 million tons (-4%). Previously, the last time that China's coal output contracted on a year-on-year basis was back in July 2021.
